name: pydantic-extra-types

metadata.name: pydantic-extra-types
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# Pydantic Extra Types

[![CI](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-extra-types/workflows/CI/badge.svg?event=push)](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-extra-types/actions?query=event%3Apush+branch%3Amain+workflow%3ACI)
[![Coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/pydantic/pydantic-extra-types/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/pydantic/pydantic-extra-types)
[![pypi](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pydantic-extra-types.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pydantic-extra-types)
[![license](https://img.shields.io/github/license/pydantic/pydantic-extra-types.svg)](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-extra-types/blob/main/LICENSE)

A place for pydantic types that probably shouldn't exist in the main pydantic lib.

See [pydantic/pydantic#5012](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic/issues/5012) for more info.

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.country
documentation: Country definitions that are based on the [ISO 3166](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_3166_country_codes).

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryAlpha2

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryAlpha2.alpha3
documentation: The country code in the [ISO 3166-1 alpha-3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-3) format.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryAlpha2.numeric_code
documentation: The country code in the [ISO 3166-1 numeric](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_numeric) format.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryAlpha2.short_name
documentation: The country short name.

CountryAlpha2 parses country codes in the [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2)
format.

```py
from pydantic import BaseModel

from pydantic_extra_types.country import CountryAlpha2

class Product(BaseModel):
    made_in: CountryAlpha2

product = Product(made_in='ES')
print(product)
#> made_in='ES'
```

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryAlpha3

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryAlpha3.alpha2
documentation: The country code in the [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2) format.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryAlpha3.numeric_code
documentation: The country code in the [ISO 3166-1 numeric](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_numeric) format.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryAlpha3.short_name
documentation: The country short name.

CountryAlpha3 parses country codes in the [ISO 3166-1 alpha-3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-3)
format.

```py
from pydantic import BaseModel

from pydantic_extra_types.country import CountryAlpha3

class Product(BaseModel):
    made_in: CountryAlpha3

product = Product(made_in="USA")
print(product)
#> made_in='USA'
```

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryNumericCode

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryNumericCode.alpha2
documentation: The country code in the [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2) format.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryNumericCode.alpha3
documentation: The country code in the [ISO 3166-1 alpha-3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-3) format.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryNumericCode.short_name
documentation: The country short name.

CountryNumericCode parses country codes in the
[ISO 3166-1 numeric](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_numeric) format.

```py
from pydantic import BaseModel

from pydantic_extra_types.country import CountryNumericCode

class Product(BaseModel):
    made_in: CountryNumericCode

product = Product(made_in="840")
print(product)
#> made_in='840'
```

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryShortName

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryShortName.alpha2
documentation: The country code in the [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2) format.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryShortName.alpha3
documentation: The country code in the [ISO 3166-1 alpha-3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-3) format.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.country.CountryShortName.numeric_code
documentation: The country code in the [ISO 3166-1 numeric](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_numeric) format.

CountryShortName parses country codes in the short name format.

```py
from pydantic import BaseModel

from pydantic_extra_types.country import CountryShortName

class Product(BaseModel):
    made_in: CountryShortName

product = Product(made_in="United States")
print(product)
#> made_in='United States'
```

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.domain
documentation:
The `domain_str` module provides the `DomainStr` data type.
This class depends on the `pydantic` package and implements custom validation for domain string format.

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.domain.DomainStr

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.domain.DomainStr.validate
documentation:
Validate a domain name from the provided value.

Args:
    __input_value: The value to be validated.
    _: The source type to be converted.

Returns:
    str: The parsed domain name.
A string subclass with custom validation for domain string format.

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.semver
documentation:
The _VersionPydanticAnnotation class provides functionality to parse and validate Semantic Versioning (SemVer) strings.

This class depends on the [semver](https://python-semver.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html) package.

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.semver._VersionPydanticAnnotation
documentation:
Represents a Semantic Versioning (SemVer).

Wraps the `version` type from `semver`.

Example:

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel

from pydantic_extra_types.semver import _VersionPydanticAnnotation

class appVersion(BaseModel):
    version: _VersionPydanticAnnotation

app_version = appVersion(version="1.2.3")

print(app_version.version)
# > 1.2.3
```

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.ulid
documentation:
The `pydantic_extra_types.ULID` module provides the [`ULID`] data type.

This class depends on the [python-ulid] package, which is a validate by the [ULID-spec](https://github.com/ulid/spec#implementations-in-other-languages).

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.ulid.ULID
documentation:
A wrapper around [python-ulid](https://pypi.org/project/python-ulid/) package, which
is a validate by the [ULID-spec](https://github.com/ulid/spec#implementations-in-other-languages).

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.payment
documentation:
The `pydantic_extra_types.payment` module provides the
[`PaymentCardNumber`][pydantic_extra_types.payment.PaymentCardNumber] data type.

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.payment.PaymentCardBrand
documentation: Payment card brands supported by the [`PaymentCardNumber`][pydantic_extra_types.payment.PaymentCardNumber].

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.payment.PaymentCardNumber

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.payment.PaymentCardNumber.validate
documentation:
Validate the `PaymentCardNumber` instance.

Args:
    __input_value: The input value to validate.
    _: The validation info.

Returns:
    The validated `PaymentCardNumber` instance.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.payment.PaymentCardNumber.masked
documentation: The masked card number.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.payment.PaymentCardNumber.validate_digits
documentation:
Validate that the card number is all digits.

Args:
    card_number: The card number to validate.

Raises:
    PydanticCustomError: If the card number is not all digits.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.payment.PaymentCardNumber.validate_luhn_check_digit
documentation:
Validate the payment card number.
Based on the [Luhn algorithm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luhn_algorithm).

Args:
    card_number: The card number to validate.

Returns:
    The validated card number.

Raises:
    PydanticCustomError: If the card number is not valid.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.payment.PaymentCardNumber.validate_brand
documentation:
Validate length based on
[BIN](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Payment_card_number#Issuer_identification_number_(IIN))
for major brands.

Args:
    card_number: The card number to validate.

Returns:
    The validated card brand.

Raises:
    PydanticCustomError: If the card number is not valid.

documentation: Whether to strip whitespace from the input value.

class_variables.name: pydantic_extra_types.payment.PaymentCardNumber.min_length
documentation: The minimum length of the card number.

class_variables.name: pydantic_extra_types.payment.PaymentCardNumber.max_length
documentation: The maximum length of the card number.

class_variables.name: pydantic_extra_types.payment.PaymentCardNumber.bin
documentation: The first 6 digits of the card number.

class_variables.name: pydantic_extra_types.payment.PaymentCardNumber.last4
documentation: The last 4 digits of the card number.

class_variables.name: pydantic_extra_types.payment.PaymentCardNumber.brand
documentation: The brand of the card.
A [payment card number](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Payment_card_number).

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.coordinate
documentation:
The `pydantic_extra_types.coordinate` module provides the [`Latitude`][pydantic_extra_types.coordinate.Latitude],
[`Longitude`][pydantic_extra_types.coordinate.Longitude], and
[`Coordinate`][pydantic_extra_types.coordinate.Coordinate] data types.

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.coordinate.Latitude
documentation:
Latitude value should be between -90 and 90, inclusive.

```py
from pydantic import BaseModel
from pydantic_extra_types.coordinate import Latitude

class Location(BaseModel):
    latitude: Latitude

location = Location(latitude=41.40338)
print(location)
#> latitude=41.40338
```

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.coordinate.Longitude
documentation:
Longitude value should be between -180 and 180, inclusive.

```py
from pydantic import BaseModel

from pydantic_extra_types.coordinate import Longitude

class Location(BaseModel):
    longitude: Longitude

location = Location(longitude=2.17403)
print(location)
#> longitude=2.17403
```

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.coordinate.Coordinate
documentation:
Coordinate parses Latitude and Longitude.

You can use the `Coordinate` data type for storing coordinates. Coordinates can be
defined using one of the following formats:

1. Tuple: `(Latitude, Longitude)`. For example: `(41.40338, 2.17403)`.
2. `Coordinate` instance: `Coordinate(latitude=Latitude, longitude=Longitude)`.

```py
from pydantic import BaseModel

from pydantic_extra_types.coordinate import Coordinate

class Location(BaseModel):
    coordinate: Coordinate

location = Location(coordinate=(41.40338, 2.17403))
#> coordinate=Coordinate(latitude=41.40338, longitude=2.17403)
```

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.phone_numbers
documentation:
The `pydantic_extra_types.phone_numbers` module provides the
[`PhoneNumber`][pydantic_extra_types.phone_numbers.PhoneNumber] data type.

This class depends on the [phonenumbers] package, which is a Python port of Google's [libphonenumber].

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.phone_numbers.PhoneNumber

class_variables.name: pydantic_extra_types.phone_numbers.PhoneNumber.supported_regions
documentation: The supported regions. If empty, all regions are supported.

class_variables.name: pydantic_extra_types.phone_numbers.PhoneNumber.default_region_code
documentation: The default region code to use when parsing phone numbers without an international prefix.

class_variables.name: pydantic_extra_types.phone_numbers.PhoneNumber.phone_format
documentation: The format of the phone number.

A wrapper around [phonenumbers](https://pypi.org/project/phonenumbers/) package, which
is a Python port of Google's [libphonenumber](https://github.com/google/libphonenumber/).

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.phone_numbers.PhoneNumberValidator
documentation:
A pydantic before validator for phone numbers using the [phonenumbers](https://pypi.org/project/phonenumbers/) package,
a Python port of Google's [libphonenumber](https://github.com/google/libphonenumber/).

Intended to be used to create custom pydantic data types using the `typing.Annotated` type construct.

Args:
    default_region (str | None): The default region code to use when parsing phone numbers without an international prefix.
        If `None` (default), the region must be supplied in the phone number as an international prefix.
    number_format (str): The format of the phone number to return. See `phonenumbers.PhoneNumberFormat` for valid values.
    supported_regions (list[str]): The supported regions. If empty, all regions are supported (default).
Returns:
    str: The formatted phone number.

Example:
    MyNumberType = Annotated[
        Union[str, phonenumbers.PhoneNumber],
        PhoneNumberValidator()
    ]
    USNumberType = Annotated[
        Union[str, phonenumbers.PhoneNumber],
        PhoneNumberValidator(supported_regions=['US'], default_region='US')
    ]

    class SomeModel(BaseModel):
        phone_number: MyNumberType
        us_number: USNumberType

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.routing_number
documentation:
The `pydantic_extra_types.routing_number` module provides the
[`ABARoutingNumber`][pydantic_extra_types.routing_number.ABARoutingNumber] data type.

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.routing_number.ABARoutingNumber

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.routing_number.ABARoutingNumber._validate_digits
documentation:
Check that the routing number is all digits.

Args:
    routing_number: The routing number to validate.

Raises:
    PydanticCustomError: If the routing number is not all digits.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.routing_number.ABARoutingNumber._validate_routing_number
documentation:
Check [digit algorithm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ABA_routing_transit_number#Check_digit) for
[ABA routing transit number](https://www.routingnumber.com/).

Args:
    routing_number: The routing number to validate.

Raises:
    PydanticCustomError: If the routing number is incorrect.

The `ABARoutingNumber` data type is a string of 9 digits representing an ABA routing transit number.

The algorithm used to validate the routing number is described in the
[ABA routing transit number](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ABA_routing_transit_number#Check_digit)
Wikipedia article.

```py
from pydantic import BaseModel

from pydantic_extra_types.routing_number import ABARoutingNumber

class BankAccount(BaseModel):
    routing_number: ABARoutingNumber

account = BankAccount(routing_number='122105155')
print(account)
#> routing_number='122105155'
```

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.isbn
documentation:
The `pydantic_extra_types.isbn` module provides functionality to recieve and validate ISBN.

ISBN (International Standard Book Number) is a numeric commercial book identifier which is intended to be unique. This module provides a ISBN type for Pydantic models.

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.isbn.isbn10_digit_calc
documentation:
Calc a ISBN-10 last digit from the provided str value. More information of validation algorithm on [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN#Check_digits)

Args:
    isbn: The str value representing the ISBN in 10 digits.

Returns:
    The calculated last digit of the ISBN-10 value.

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.isbn.isbn13_digit_calc
documentation:
Calc a ISBN-13 last digit from the provided str value. More information of validation algorithm on [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN#Check_digits)

Args:
    isbn: The str value representing the ISBN in 13 digits.

Returns:
    The calculated last digit of the ISBN-13 value.

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.isbn.ISBN

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.isbn.ISBN.__get_pydantic_core_schema__
documentation:
Return a Pydantic CoreSchema with the ISBN validation.

Args:
    source: The source type to be converted.
    handler: The handler to get the CoreSchema.

Returns:
    A Pydantic CoreSchema with the ISBN validation.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.isbn.ISBN._validate
documentation:
Validate a ISBN from the provided str value.

Args:
    __input_value: The str value to be validated.
    _: The source type to be converted.

Returns:
    The validated ISBN.

Raises:
    PydanticCustomError: If the ISBN is not valid.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.isbn.ISBN.validate_isbn_format
documentation:
Validate a ISBN format from the provided str value.

Args:
    value: The str value representing the ISBN in 10 or 13 digits.

Raises:
    PydanticCustomError: If the ISBN is not valid.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.isbn.ISBN.convert_isbn10_to_isbn13
documentation:
Convert an ISBN-10 to ISBN-13.

Args:
    value: The ISBN-10 value to be converted.

Returns:
    The converted ISBN or the original value if no conversion is necessary.

Represents a ISBN and provides methods for conversion, validation, and serialization.

```py
from pydantic import BaseModel

from pydantic_extra_types.isbn import ISBN


class Book(BaseModel):
    isbn: ISBN

book = Book(isbn="8537809667")
print(book)
#> isbn='9788537809662'
```

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.timezone_name
documentation: Time zone name validation and serialization module.

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.timezone_name._is_available
documentation: Check if a module is available for import.

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.timezone_name._tz_provider_from_zone_info
documentation: Get timezones from the zoneinfo module.

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.timezone_name._tz_provider_from_pytz
documentation: Get timezones from the pytz module.

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.timezone_name._warn_about_pytz_usage
documentation: Warn about using pytz with Python 3.9 or later.

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.timezone_name.get_timezones
documentation: Determine the timezone provider and return available timezones.

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.timezone_name.TimeZoneName

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.timezone_name.TimeZoneName._validate
documentation:
Validate a time zone name from the provided str value.

Args:
    __input_value: The str value to be validated.
    _: The Pydantic ValidationInfo.

Returns:
    The validated time zone name.

Raises:
    PydanticCustomError: If the timezone name is not valid.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.timezone_name.TimeZoneName.__get_pydantic_core_schema__
documentation:
Return a Pydantic CoreSchema with the timezone name validation.

Args:
    _: The source type.
    __: The handler to get the CoreSchema.

Returns:
    A Pydantic CoreSchema with the timezone name validation.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.timezone_name.TimeZoneName.__get_pydantic_json_schema__
documentation:
Return a Pydantic JSON Schema with the timezone name validation.

Args:
    schema: The Pydantic CoreSchema.
    handler: The handler to get the JSON Schema.

Returns:
    A Pydantic JSON Schema with the timezone name validation.

TimeZoneName is a custom string subclass for validating and serializing timezone names.

The TimeZoneName class uses the IANA Time Zone Database for validation.
It supports both strict and non-strict modes for timezone name validation.


## Examples:

Some examples of using the TimeZoneName class:

### Normal usage:

```python
from pydantic_extra_types.timezone_name import TimeZoneName
from pydantic import BaseModel
class Location(BaseModel):
    city: str
    timezone: TimeZoneName

loc = Location(city="New York", timezone="America/New_York")
print(loc.timezone)

>> America/New_York

```

### Non-strict mode:

```python

from pydantic_extra_types.timezone_name import TimeZoneName, timezone_name_settings

@timezone_name_settings(strict=False)
class TZNonStrict(TimeZoneName):
    pass

tz = TZNonStrict("america/new_york")

print(tz)

>> america/new_york

```

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.mac_address
documentation:
The MAC address module provides functionality to parse and validate MAC addresses in different
formats, such as IEEE 802 MAC-48, EUI-48, EUI-64, or a 20-octet format.

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.mac_address.MacAddress

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.mac_address.MacAddress.__get_pydantic_core_schema__
documentation:
Return a Pydantic CoreSchema with the MAC address validation.

Args:
    source: The source type to be converted.
    handler: The handler to get the CoreSchema.

Returns:
    A Pydantic CoreSchema with the MAC address validation.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.mac_address.MacAddress._validate
documentation:
Validate a MAC Address from the provided str value.

Args:
    __input_value: The str value to be validated.
    _: The source type to be converted.

Returns:
    str: The parsed MAC address.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.mac_address.MacAddress.validate_mac_address
documentation: Validate a MAC Address from the provided byte value.

Represents a MAC address and provides methods for conversion, validation, and serialization.

```py
from pydantic import BaseModel

from pydantic_extra_types.mac_address import MacAddress


class Network(BaseModel):
    mac_address: MacAddress


network = Network(mac_address="00:00:5e:00:53:01")
print(network)
#> mac_address='00:00:5e:00:53:01'
```

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.s3
documentation:
The `pydantic_extra_types.s3` module provides the
[`S3Path`][pydantic_extra_types.s3.S3Path] data type.

A simpleAWS S3 URLs parser.
It also provides the `Bucket`, `Key` component.

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.s3.S3Path
documentation:
An object representing a valid S3 path.
This type also allows you to access the `bucket` and `key` component of the S3 path.
It also contains the `last_key` which represents the last part of the path (tipically a file).

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel
from pydantic_extra_types.s3 import S3Path

class TestModel(BaseModel):
    path: S3Path

p = 's3://my-data-bucket/2023/08/29/sales-report.csv'
model = TestModel(path=p)
model

#> TestModel(path=S3Path('s3://my-data-bucket/2023/08/29/sales-report.csv'))

model.path.bucket

#> 'my-data-bucket'

```

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.semantic_version
documentation: SemanticVersion definition that is based on the Semantiv Versioning Specification [semver](https://semver.org/).

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.semantic_version.SemanticVersion
documentation: Semantic version based on the official [semver thread](https://python-semver.readthedocs.io/en/latest/advanced/combine-pydantic-and-semver.html).

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.color
documentation:
Color definitions are used as per the CSS3
[CSS Color Module Level 3](http://www.w3.org/TR/css3-color/#svg-color) specification.

A few colors have multiple names referring to the sames colors, eg. `grey` and `gray` or `aqua` and `cyan`.

In these cases the _last_ color when sorted alphabetically takes preferences,
eg. `Color((0, 255, 255)).as_named() == 'cyan'` because "cyan" comes after "aqua".

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.parse_tuple
documentation:
Parse a tuple or list to get RGBA values.

Args:
    value: A tuple or list.

Returns:
    An `RGBA` tuple parsed from the input tuple.

Raises:
    PydanticCustomError: If tuple is not valid.

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.parse_str
documentation:
Parse a string representing a color to an RGBA tuple.

Possible formats for the input string include:

* named color, see `COLORS_BY_NAME`
* hex short eg. `<prefix>fff` (prefix can be `#`, `0x` or nothing)
* hex long eg. `<prefix>ffffff` (prefix can be `#`, `0x` or nothing)
* `rgb(<r>, <g>, <b>)`
* `rgba(<r>, <g>, <b>, <a>)`
* `transparent`

Args:
    value: A string representing a color.

Returns:
    An `RGBA` tuple parsed from the input string.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the input string cannot be parsed to an RGBA tuple.

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.ints_to_rgba
documentation:
Converts integer or string values for RGB color and an optional alpha value to an `RGBA` object.

Args:
    r: An integer or string representing the red color value.
    g: An integer or string representing the green color value.
    b: An integer or string representing the blue color value.
    alpha: A float representing the alpha value. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    An instance of the `RGBA` class with the corresponding color and alpha values.

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.parse_color_value
documentation:
Parse the color value provided and return a number between 0 and 1.

Args:
    value: An integer or string color value.
    max_val: Maximum range value. Defaults to 255.

Raises:
    PydanticCustomError: If the value is not a valid color.

Returns:
    A number between 0 and 1.

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.parse_float_alpha
documentation:
Parse an alpha value checking it's a valid float in the range 0 to 1.

Args:
    value: The input value to parse.

Returns:
    The parsed value as a float, or `None` if the value was None or equal 1.

Raises:
    PydanticCustomError: If the input value cannot be successfully parsed as a float in the expected range.

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.parse_hsl
documentation:
Parse raw hue, saturation, lightness, and alpha values and convert to RGBA.

Args:
    h: The hue value.
    h_units: The unit for hue value.
    sat: The saturation value.
    light: The lightness value.
    alpha: Alpha value.

Returns:
    An instance of `RGBA`.

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.float_to_255
documentation:
Converts a float value between 0 and 1 (inclusive) to an integer between 0 and 255 (inclusive).

Args:
    c: The float value to be converted. Must be between 0 and 1 (inclusive).

Returns:
    The integer equivalent of the given float value rounded to the nearest whole number.

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.RGBA
documentation: Internal use only as a representation of a color.

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.Color

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.Color.original
documentation: Original value passed to `Color`.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.Color.as_named
documentation:
Returns the name of the color if it can be found in `COLORS_BY_VALUE` dictionary,
otherwise returns the hexadecimal representation of the color or raises `ValueError`.

Args:
    fallback: If True, falls back to returning the hexadecimal representation of
        the color instead of raising a ValueError when no named color is found.

Returns:
    The name of the color, or the hexadecimal representation of the color.

Raises:
    ValueError: When no named color is found and fallback is `False`.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.Color.as_hex
documentation:
Returns the hexadecimal representation of the color.

Hex string representing the color can be 3, 4, 6, or 8 characters depending on whether the string
a "short" representation of the color is possible and whether there's an alpha channel.

Returns:
    The hexadecimal representation of the color.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.Color.as_rgb
documentation: Color as an `rgb(<r>, <g>, <b>)` or `rgba(<r>, <g>, <b>, <a>)` string.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.Color.as_rgb_tuple
documentation:
Returns the color as an RGB or RGBA tuple.

Args:
    alpha: Whether to include the alpha channel. There are three options for this input:

        - `None` (default): Include alpha only if it's set. (e.g. not `None`)
        - `True`: Always include alpha.
        - `False`: Always omit alpha.

Returns:
    A tuple that contains the values of the red, green, and blue channels in the range 0 to 255.
        If alpha is included, it is in the range 0 to 1.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.Color.as_hsl
documentation: Color as an `hsl(<h>, <s>, <l>)` or `hsl(<h>, <s>, <l>, <a>)` string.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.color.Color.as_hsl_tuple
documentation:
Returns the color as an HSL or HSLA tuple.

Args:
    alpha: Whether to include the alpha channel.

        - `None` (default): Include the alpha channel only if it's set (e.g. not `None`).
        - `True`: Always include alpha.
        - `False`: Always omit alpha.

Returns:
    The color as a tuple of hue, saturation, lightness, and alpha (if included).
        All elements are in the range 0 to 1.

Note:
    This is HSL as used in HTML and most other places, not HLS as used in Python's `colorsys`.
Represents a color.

ISO_15924 parses script in the [ISO 15924](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_15924)
format.

```py
from pydantic import BaseModel

from pydantic_extra_types.language_code import ISO_15924

class Script(BaseModel):
    alpha_4: ISO_15924

script = Script(alpha_4='Java')
print(lang)
# > script='Java'
```

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.pendulum_dt
documentation:
Native Pendulum DateTime object implementation. This is a copy of the Pendulum DateTime object, but with a Pydantic
CoreSchema implementation. This allows Pydantic to validate the DateTime object.

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.pendulum_dt.DateTime

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.pendulum_dt.DateTime.__get_pydantic_core_schema__
documentation:
Return a Pydantic CoreSchema with the Datetime validation

Args:
    source: The source type to be converted.
    handler: The handler to get the CoreSchema.

Returns:
    A Pydantic CoreSchema with the Datetime validation.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.pendulum_dt.DateTime._validate
documentation:
Validate the datetime object and return it.

Args:
    value: The value to validate.
    handler: The handler to get the CoreSchema.

Returns:
    The validated value or raises a PydanticCustomError.

A `pendulum.DateTime` object. At runtime, this type decomposes into pendulum.DateTime automatically.
This type exists because Pydantic throws a fit on unknown types.

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel
from pydantic_extra_types.pendulum_dt import DateTime

class test_model(BaseModel):
    dt: DateTime

print(test_model(dt='2021-01-01T00:00:00+00:00'))

#> test_model(dt=DateTime(2021, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, tzinfo=FixedTimezone(0, name="+00:00")))
```

A `pendulum.Date` object. At runtime, this type decomposes into pendulum.Date automatically.
This type exists because Pydantic throws a fit on unknown types.

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel
from pydantic_extra_types.pendulum_dt import Date

class test_model(BaseModel):
    dt: Date

print(test_model(dt='2021-01-01'))

#> test_model(dt=Date(2021, 1, 1))
```

A `pendulum.Duration` object. At runtime, this type decomposes into pendulum.Duration automatically.
This type exists because Pydantic throws a fit on unknown types.

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel
from pydantic_extra_types.pendulum_dt import Duration

class test_model(BaseModel):
    delta_t: Duration

print(test_model(delta_t='P1DT25H'))

#> test_model(delta_t=Duration(days=2, hours=1))
```

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.currency_code
documentation: Currency definitions that are based on the [ISO4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.currency_code.ISO4217

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.currency_code.ISO4217._validate
documentation:
Validate a ISO 4217 language code from the provided str value.

Args:
    currency_code: The str value to be validated.
    _: The Pydantic ValidationInfo.

Returns:
    The validated ISO 4217 currency code.

Raises:
    PydanticCustomError: If the ISO 4217 currency code is not valid.

ISO4217 parses Currency in the [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) format.

```py
from pydantic import BaseModel

from pydantic_extra_types.currency_code import ISO4217

class Currency(BaseModel):
    alpha_3: ISO4217

currency = Currency(alpha_3='AED')
print(currency)
# > alpha_3='AED'
```

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.currency_code.Currency

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.currency_code.Currency._validate
documentation:
Validate a subset of the [ISO4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) format.
It excludes bonds testing codes and precious metals.

Args:
    currency_symbol: The str value to be validated.
    _: The Pydantic ValidationInfo.

Returns:
    The validated ISO 4217 currency code.

Raises:
    PydanticCustomError: If the ISO 4217 currency code is not valid or is bond, precious metal or testing code.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.currency_code.Currency.__get_pydantic_core_schema__
documentation:
Return a Pydantic CoreSchema with the currency subset of the
[ISO4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) format.
It excludes bonds testing codes and precious metals.

 Args:
     _: The source type.
     __: The handler to get the CoreSchema.

 Returns:
    A Pydantic CoreSchema with the subset of the currency subset of the
    [ISO4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) format.
    It excludes bonds testing codes and precious metals.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.currency_code.Currency.__get_pydantic_json_schema__
documentation:
Return a Pydantic JSON Schema with subset of the [ISO4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) format.
Excluding bonds testing codes and precious metals.

Args:
    schema: The Pydantic CoreSchema.
    handler: The handler to get the JSON Schema.

Returns:
    A Pydantic JSON Schema with the subset of the ISO4217 currency code validation. without bonds testing codes
    and precious metals.

Currency parses currency subset of the [ISO 4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) format.
It excludes bonds testing codes and precious metals.
    ```py
    from pydantic import BaseModel

    from pydantic_extra_types.currency_code import Currency

    class currency(BaseModel):
        alpha_3: Currency

    cur = currency(alpha_3='AED')
    print(cur)
    # > alpha_3='AED'
    ```

modules.name: pydantic_extra_types.language_code

Return a list of LanguageInfo objects containing the language information.

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.language_code._index_by_alpha3

functions.name: pydantic_extra_types.language_code._index_by_name
documentation: Return a dictionary with the language name as the key and the LanguageInfo object as the value.

classes.name: pydantic_extra_types.language_code.LanguageInfo
documentation:
LanguageInfo is a dataclass that contains the language information.

Args:
    alpha2: The language code in the [ISO 639-1 alpha-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_639-1) format.
    alpha3: The language code in the [ISO 639-3 alpha-3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_639-3) format.
    name: The language name.

documentation:
Validate a language code in the ISO 639-1 alpha-2 format from the provided str value.

Args:
    __input_value: The str value to be validated.
    _: The Pydantic ValidationInfo.

Returns:
    The validated language code in the ISO 639-1 alpha-2 format.

documentation:
Return a Pydantic CoreSchema with the language code in the ISO 639-1 alpha-2 format validation.

Args:
    source: The source type.
    handler: The handler to get the CoreSchema.

Returns:
    A Pydantic CoreSchema with the language code in the ISO 639-1 alpha-2 format validation.

documentation:

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.language_code.LanguageAlpha2.alpha3
documentation: The language code in the [ISO 639-3 alpha-3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_639-3) format.

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.language_code.LanguageAlpha2.name
documentation: The language name.

LanguageAlpha2 parses languages codes in the [ISO 639-1 alpha-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_639-1)
format.

```py
from pydantic import BaseModel

from pydantic_extra_types.language_code import LanguageAlpha2

class Movie(BaseModel):
    audio_lang: LanguageAlpha2
    subtitles_lang: LanguageAlpha2

movie = Movie(audio_lang='de', subtitles_lang='fr')
print(movie)
#> audio_lang='de' subtitles_lang='fr'
```

methods.name: pydantic_extra_types.language_code.LanguageName.alpha3
documentation: The language code in the [ISO 639-3 alpha-3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_639-3) format.

LanguageName parses languages names listed in the [ISO 639-3 standard](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_639-3)
format.

```py
from pydantic import BaseModel

from pydantic_extra_types.language_code import LanguageName

class Movie(BaseModel):
    audio_lang: LanguageName
    subtitles_lang: LanguageName

movie = Movie(audio_lang='Dutch', subtitles_lang='Mandarin Chinese')
print(movie)
#> audio_lang='Dutch' subtitles_lang='Mandarin Chinese'
```
